setup.title: Welcome to MoonlightWeb
setup.intro: Let's finish setting up MoonlightWeb on this computer.
setup.internetBody: MoonlightWeb can publish a secure public link (e.g. https://a1b2c3d4.moonlightweb.top) so you can stream from outside your local network.
setup.sunshinePaired: Sunshine is installed and paired with MoonlightWeb.
setup.sunshineInstalled: Sunshine is already installed. Enter its credentials so MoonlightWeb can pair with it.
setup.sunshineNotDetected: Sunshine was not detected. Install it automatically, then set the credentials MoonlightWeb will use to pair.
setup.sunshineManual: Sunshine was not detected. Please install Sunshine manually, then reopen this page to pair.
setup.credsRequired: Please enter the Sunshine username and password so MoonlightWeb can pair automatically.
setup.autostartOption: Launch MoonlightWeb automatically when you log in
setup.autostartInstalled: MoonlightWeb already starts automatically at login.
setup.donePermissions: One last step: open Sunshine and grant it Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions in System Settings — macOS requires you to do this manually.
setup.sunshineInstallFailed: Sunshine installation failed: {{error}}. Try again (you'll be asked for your password), uncheck Sunshine, or skip this step.

hosts.title: App Library
hosts.emptyHint: Click "Scan Network" or add a host manually
hosts.noHostReturned: No host was returned from the server
hosts.remoteAccess: Other computers can connect to this server at:

secure.title: Secure connection required
secure.explain: You opened MoonlightWeb over an unencrypted connection. Streaming needs a secure (HTTPS) page. Click below to reopen it securely — your browser will ask you to accept the local certificate once.

apps.emptyHint: Add applications in Sunshine on the host
apps.loadFailedHint: The host is unreachable or took too long to respond

login.pinSubtitle: Enter the PIN shown on the server admin page
login.certSubtitle: Upload your certificate file to authenticate
login.pinHint: Access the admin page from localhost to view the PIN
login.invalidCert: Invalid certificate. Check that you have the correct file.

pairing.title: Pair with {{name}}
pairing.instruction: Enter this PIN in Sunshine to authorize the client:
pairing.enterPin: Enter this PIN in Sunshine (Web UI or stdin on the host)

settings.title: Streaming Settings
settings.resolutionDesc: Resolution of the stream sent over the network.
settings.frameRateDesc: A higher frame rate helps reduce latency but needs more bandwidth.
settings.hdrDesc: High Dynamic Range — richer colors and contrast.
settings.hdrNoWebgpu: Unavailable: this browser/device has no WebGPU, the stream stays SDR.
settings.chroma444Desc: Full-resolution color (4:4:4) instead of 4:2:0. Requires both the host AND client GPU to support 4:4:4 for the chosen codec. Needs significantly higher bandwidth.
settings.tearingDesc: Disables VSync for slightly lower latency; may cause image tearing.
settings.muteHostDesc: Mute the host PC speakers while streaming. Audio is still sent to this device.
settings.bitrateDesc: Auto-adjusted from resolution, frame rate and HDR.
settings.videoEnhancementDesc: Leverages hardware acceleration to improve picture clarity by upscaling when stream resolution is below your display resolution.
settings.webgpuUnavailable: WebGPU is not available in this browser — video enhancement is disabled.
settings.videoEnhancementHdrNote: With HDR, the stream is tone-mapped to SDR (ACES) before enhancement — uses software AV1 decoding, slightly heavier on the CPU.
settings.videoCodecDesc: HEVC for best balance of quality and compatibility.
settings.codecFallback: {{selected}} was selected but is not supported by this browser. Falling back to {{effective}}.
settings.noCodecSupportedBody: This browser does not support H.264, HEVC, or AV1 decoding. Streaming is not possible.
settings.showPerfStatsDesc: Overlays FPS, bitrate, frame loss and latency stats during streaming.
settings.gamingModeDesc: Captures the mouse and every keystroke — even the Windows key — for a native-like experience. Only the exit shortcut stays on this device.
settings.touchScreenDesc: Tap directly where you want on the picture (absolute) instead of the relative trackpad model.
settings.pointerSensitivityDesc: Trackpad/touch cursor speed (higher = faster pointer movement).
settings.resetDefaultsDesc: Restore all streaming settings to their default values.
settings.powerSaveDesc: Forces the lightest pipeline on battery: native video transport (UDP first), H.264, no HDR, no enhancement, 720p / 60fps. Resolution, frame rate and bitrate stay adjustable. Unchecking restores your previous values if you didn't change them.
settings.languageDesc: Interface language. The page reloads when you change it.

admin.title: Server Settings
admin.accessPinDesc: Share this PIN with remote users to grant them access to this server. The PIN is valid for one session only.
admin.pinConsumedHint: The previous PIN was used by a remote user. Generate a new PIN to allow another session.
admin.activeSessionsDesc: Remote users currently connected to this server.
admin.certAuthDesc: When enabled, remote users can upload a certificate file instead of entering a PIN. The certificate is a long random token that never changes — generate it once and share it securely.
admin.downloadCertHint: The certificate grants the same access as a PIN. Share it only with trusted users. If compromised, use the "Generate" button below to issue a new one.
admin.internetInfo1: By enabling Internet Access, you authorize sending this server's public IP address to PowerDNS to create an A record pointing to your network.
admin.upnpAvailableNote: Authorizes UPnP port mapping on your router (available on this network). You can also forward ports manually.
admin.upnpUnavailableNote: Authorizes UPnP port mapping on your router (not available on this network). You can also forward ports manually.
admin.dnsPropagating: DNS propagation in progress — your site will be available shortly. This may take a few minutes...
admin.portMappingHint: UPnP is not available on your router. To allow external access, manually forward ports in your router settings:
admin.portMappingEnterIp: Enter the LAN IP address of this server (e.g. 192.168.1.x) as the destination.
admin.localAccessHint: Access this server from other devices on your local network.
admin.transportModeHint: When set to Auto, the system tries the best available protocol (UDP > TCP > WSS) based on network conditions and browser support.
admin.httpsPortDesc: Configure the server HTTPS port. Changes require a brief service interruption while the port is rebound.
admin.confirmRegenCert: Regenerating the certificate will invalidate all existing certificate files. Continue?
admin.portSavedRestart: Port saved to settings. Restart required to apply.
admin.noPinToCopy: No valid PIN to copy — generate one first
admin.pinSelected: PIN selected — press Ctrl+C to copy
admin.dnsPropagated: DNS propagated -- your site is now available at {{domain}}
admin.dnsTimeout: DNS propagation timed out after 5 minutes. Check your domain configuration.
admin.internetNotActive: Internet access is enabled but not active: DNS registration did not complete. Check the PowerDNS token and your network, then re-enable Internet access.
admin.sunshineInstalled: Sunshine is installed on this computer.
admin.sunshineNotInstalled: Sunshine is not installed. Install it to stream games from this computer.
admin.sunshineManual: Sunshine is not installed, and automatic installation is not available on this system. Please install Sunshine manually.
admin.sunshineInstallHint: Your operating system will ask for your account password to install Sunshine.
admin.sunshineCredsRequired: Enter a username and password for Sunshine.
admin.stopSunshineHint: Stops the Sunshine server running on this computer. On macOS it also lives in the top-right menu bar. It restarts at your next login (or launch it from Applications).
admin.startSunshineHint: Starts the Sunshine server on this computer so you can stream from it.
admin.sunshineStopped2: Sunshine is installed but not running.

stream.degradeBitrate: Unstable network — bitrate reduced to {{mbps}} Mbps
stream.degradeBitrateFps: Unstable network — bitrate reduced to {{mbps}} Mbps, capped at 60 fps
stream.degradeMedia: Unstable network — switching to the native video transport
stream.degradeRestart: Unstable network — restarting the video transport
stream.takenOverBody: This session was taken over by another device.
stream.revokedBody: This device's access was revoked by the administrator.
stream.iosFullscreenHint: For true fullscreen: Share → Add to Home Screen, then launch Moonlight from the icon.

transport.allFailed: Connection failed — all transport modes failed

launch.hevcH264Unsupported: HEVC and H.264 both unsupported by browser
launch.hdrFallback: HDR unavailable — switched to {{to}} without HDR

screenRecording.title: Screen recording permission needed
screenRecording.body: Sunshine on "{{name}}" can't capture the screen yet. On macOS, open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording and turn on Sunshine, then use "Restart Sunshine & retry" below (macOS only applies the permission after Sunshine restarts). If there's no display, connect one or use an HDMI dummy plug.
screenRecording.opened: Opened Screen Recording settings on the host.
screenRecording.openFailed: Couldn't open the settings pane — open it manually.
screenRecording.restartFailed: Couldn't restart Sunshine — quit and reopen it manually.
